title: Range value: invariant enforcement via sealed construction + test gaps
analysis:
Value::Range variant has public fields (inherent in Rust enums), allowing direct struct-literal construction that bypasses the normalizing Value::range() constructor. The constructor normalizes inclusivity flags (lower_inclusive=false when lower is None, etc.) to ensure logically identical ranges compare equal and hash identically.

Currently, Display, PartialEq, content_hash, and Ord all have debug_assert! checks that catch invariant violations in debug builds, but use raw (un-normalized) flags for computation. In release builds, debug_assert! is a no-op, so bypassed construction produces:
- Wrong brackets in Display (e.g., '[-inf..' instead of '(-inf..')
- Broken equality for logically identical ranges
- Different content hashes for semantically equal ranges
- Inconsistent ordering

The GUI format_value function (gui/src-tauri/src/types.rs:163) has the same bug — uses raw flags without normalization.

Approach: Replace debug_assert!-only defense with defensive re-normalization at each use site. The pattern `*flag && bound.is_some()` (already in the constructor at line 118-119) is applied inline in Display, PartialEq, content_hash, and Ord. This makes the code self-healing: bypassed construction produces correct results everywhere.

Existing #[cfg(debug_assertions)] #[should_panic] tests (6 total) are replaced with tests that verify correct normalized behavior for bypassed construction.

Three test gaps are also filled:
1. content_hash equivalence for both-None Range with different inclusivity flags
2. PartialEq for the common both-bounds-present case
3. content_hash for the common both-bounds-present case

Additional review fix: MockGeometryKernel::find_ops and has_op hold MutexGuard across caller-supplied closure execution. If the closure panics, the mutex is poisoned, causing all subsequent operations to fail with misleading 'poisoned' errors. Fix: snapshot operations into a local Vec while holding the lock, release, then filter/search the snapshot.

prerequisites.description: Add gap test: value_range_hash_both_none_ignores_inclusive — content_hash equivalence for Range(None, None) with different inclusivity flags (true,true vs false,false). Uses Value::range() so normalization ensures equality. Passes immediately.

Add gap test: value_range_eq_both_bounds_present — PartialEq for the most common case: Range with both lower and upper bounds present ([0..10] == [0..10], [0..10] != [0..20]). Uses Value::range(). Passes immediately.

Add gap test: value_range_hash_both_bounds_present — content_hash for both-bounds Range. Same bounds produce same hash; different bounds produce different hash. Uses Value::range(). Passes immediately.

steps.description: Replace #[should_panic] Display bypass tests with normalization-verifying tests. Remove value_range_invariant_bypass_lower_none_inclusive_display and value_range_invariant_bypass_upper_none_inclusive_display. Add value_range_bypass_display_renormalizes_lower (constructs Range{lower:None, lower_inclusive:true, ..} via struct literal, asserts Display outputs '(' not '[') and value_range_bypass_display_renormalizes_upper (constructs Range{upper:None, upper_inclusive:true, ..}, asserts ')' not ']'). New tests FAIL because Display still uses debug_assert! which panics before reaching bracket output.

Add defensive re-normalization in Display::fmt for Value::Range (value.rs ~line 792). Replace the debug_assert! block with inline normalization: `let lower_inclusive = *lower_inclusive && lower.is_some(); let upper_inclusive = *upper_inclusive && upper.is_some();` then use these normalized locals for bracket selection. Step-1 tests pass.

Replace #[should_panic] PartialEq bypass test with normalization-verifying test. Remove value_range_invariant_bypass_lower_none_inclusive_eq. Add value_range_bypass_eq_renormalizes (constructs two Range struct literals: one with lower:None+lower_inclusive:true and one with lower:None+lower_inclusive:false, both having same upper bound; asserts they compare equal). FAILS because PartialEq still uses debug_assert! which panics.

Add defensive re-normalization in PartialEq::eq for Value::Range (value.rs ~line 459). Replace the debug_assert! blocks with inline normalization of all four flags: `let ali = *ali && al.is_some()` etc. Compare normalized flags instead of raw flags. Step-3 test passes.

Replace #[should_panic] content_hash bypass tests with normalization-verifying tests. Remove value_range_invariant_bypass_lower_none_inclusive_content_hash and value_range_invariant_bypass_upper_none_inclusive_content_hash. Add value_range_bypass_hash_renormalizes (constructs Range struct literal with lower:None+lower_inclusive:true, asserts its content_hash equals Value::range(None, same_upper, false, false).content_hash()). FAILS because content_hash still uses debug_assert! which panics.

Add defensive re-normalization in Value::content_hash() for the Range arm (value.rs ~line 350). Replace the debug_assert! blocks with inline normalization: `let lower_inclusive = *lower_inclusive && lower.is_some(); let upper_inclusive = *upper_inclusive && upper.is_some();` then hash the normalized flags. Step-5 test passes.

Replace #[should_panic] Ord bypass test with normalization-verifying test. Remove value_range_invariant_bypass_lower_none_inclusive_cmp. Add value_range_bypass_cmp_renormalizes (constructs two Range struct literals with lower:None and different lower_inclusive flags, asserts cmp returns Equal). FAILS because Ord still uses debug_assert! which panics.
Add defensive re-normalization in Ord::cmp for Value::Range (value.rs ~line 602). Replace the debug_assert! blocks with inline normalization of all four flags before comparison. Step-7 test passes.

Fix GUI format_value in gui/src-tauri/src/types.rs (~line 163). Add defensive re-normalization: `let lower_inclusive = *lower_inclusive && lower.is_some(); let upper_inclusive = *upper_inclusive && upper.is_some();` before bracket selection. No dedicated test (GUI crate lacks test infrastructure; pattern identical to Display fix verified in step-1/2).

Rebase task/364 onto main to resolve merge_ff_failed. Main advanced with task-status auto-commits (tasks.json, orchestrator.yaml, .gitignore) since branch diverged at 8af9b4da. No source-code conflicts expected — main only changed orchestrator metadata files, while this branch changed value.rs, types.rs, and lib.rs. Accept main's version for .task/ files (plan.json will be rewritten after rebase). Run full test suite after rebase to verify no regressions.

Rebase task/364 onto current main to resolve second merge_ff_failed. Main advanced 7 commits (all task-status auto-commits) after step-10's rebase. All task/364 commits are already ancestors of main (0 unique commits on branch), so this is a pure fast-forward rebase — `git rebase main` moves the branch pointer to main's tip with no conflicts. Verify with `git log --oneline main..HEAD` showing the task commits on top, and run `cargo test -p reify-types` to confirm no regressions.

Write test mock_find_ops_does_not_poison_mutex_on_closure_panic in crates/reify-test-support/src/mocks.rs. The test creates a MockGeometryKernel, executes a Sphere op, then calls find_ops with a closure that panics — wrapped in std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(...)). After catching the panic, it asserts kernel.op_count() == 1 and kernel.last_op().is_some() succeed without panic. Write a second test mock_has_op_does_not_poison_mutex_on_closure_panic with the same pattern for has_op. Both tests FAIL because the panic inside find_ops/has_op poisons the mutex, so the subsequent op_count()/last_op() calls panic with 'poisoned lock'.

Fix find_ops (mocks.rs:283-291): snapshot operations into a local Vec while holding the lock, release the lock, then filter the snapshot. Change from `self.operations.lock().unwrap().iter().filter(|rec| f(&rec.op)).cloned().collect()` to `let ops = self.operations.lock().unwrap().clone(); ops.iter().filter(|rec| f(&rec.op)).cloned().collect()`. Apply the same pattern to has_op (mocks.rs:299-305): `let ops = self.operations.lock().unwrap().clone(); ops.iter().any(|rec| f(&rec.op))`. Step-12 tests pass because the mutex is released before closure execution.

Rebase task/364 onto current main to resolve merge_ff_failed. Main is 46 commits ahead (task-status auto-commits, task-203 LSP hover merge, task-351 merge, syntax doc-comment changes, eval trait-conformance tests, test-support fixtures). No source-code conflicts expected — main did not touch mocks.rs, while this branch's new changes (steps 12-13) are only in mocks.rs. Run `cargo test -p reify-test-support` after rebase to confirm no regressions.

design_decisions.rationale: Sealing Value::Range would require converting the enum variant to a struct with private fields, changing every pattern match across 6+ files (value.rs, gui/types.rs, lsp/analysis.rs, constraints/classifier.rs, test-support/values.rs, test-support/builders.rs). Re-normalization at use sites is a cheap, localized fix with the same safety guarantee: bypassed construction always produces correct results.

design_decisions.decision: Remove debug_assert! in favor of silent re-normalization
design_decisions.rationale: debug_assert! only catches bugs in debug builds and provides no protection in release. Defensive normalization makes the code self-healing in all build modes. The Value::range() constructor remains the documented API, and the normalization at use sites serves as defense-in-depth without runtime cost (two bool ANDs).

The existing tests verified that debug_assert! fired for invariant violations. With normalization replacing debug_assert!, these tests must be replaced with tests that verify correct normalized behavior. The replacement tests provide stronger guarantees: they verify correct output, not just panic detection.

design_decisions.decision: GUI format_value fix without dedicated test
design_decisions.rationale: The gui/src-tauri crate has no test module and depends on Tauri. Adding test infrastructure would be disproportionate to the fix. The code change is a one-line pattern identical to the Display fix, which has thorough test coverage in value.rs.

design_decisions.decision: Snapshot-then-filter for mutex safety in mock kernel
design_decisions.rationale: Cloning the Vec<GeometryOpRecord> before running caller-supplied closures ensures the MutexGuard is dropped before any user code executes. The clone cost is negligible in test code — these are mock operations with small payloads. This prevents mutex poisoning if a closure panics (e.g., from an assertion failure or unwrap), so subsequent test operations get the real error, not a misleading 'poisoned lock' message.

reuse.how: Apply the same two-expression normalization inline at each use site (Display, PartialEq, content_hash, Ord, GUI format_value)

reuse.what: Existing Range test infrastructure (make_range helper, test patterns)
reuse.how: Follow the same test structure for new gap tests; reuse Value::range() and direct struct-literal patterns for bypass tests
Not needed directly (these tests use Value::Int/range directly), but the pattern of dedicated test constructors is consistent with project conventions
New mutex-poisoning tests follow the same kernel setup pattern (create kernel, execute ops), then add catch_unwind + post-panic assertions
